description:
Cranelift's native CLIF writer reserves a wide left gutter for the @<hex> srcloc column. Lines with srclocs sit at col 0; lines without are padded out past the gutter (~32 cols), so block headers and constant-folded ops splay right while annotated insts hug the margin. The fz dump --emit clif output inherits this and is hard to read.

Rewrite the post-processing pass (annotate_srclocs -> format_clif in src/main.rs) to:
- strip Cranelift's gutter,
- re-indent: function header / closing brace / block headers at col 0; sig/fn decls and instructions at col 4,
- decode the @<hex> prefix into @line:col,
- fold the srcloc into a single trailing comment aligned to a fixed body column, merging any existing Cranelift const-prop hint (; vN = K) into the same comment block.

## Acceptance

- fz dump --emit clif fixtures/add1.fz visually clean.
- fz_dump_emits_clif and expect_clif_demo.fz fixture check updated to match new srcloc syntax (; @L:).
- cargo test green.
- One commit.
title: Readable fz dump CLIF layout
